reasoning: Q1 is executing correctly at 79.2 ms, competitive with DuckDB (86.4 ms best) but with clear optimization opportunities. The 6-group aggregation is a degenerate case perfect for direct array indexing, and lock contention on the final merge can be eliminated with compact hash tables and array-based approach. Selecting all low-risk optimizations (array aggregation, compact hash table, loop fusion) plus medium-risk SIMD filtering can yield 15-25% speedup to reach ~60-65 ms, matching or beating DuckDB.
focus_areas: Implement histogram/adaptive aggregation with direct array indexing for 6-group case (priority 6, 6-10% gain, low risk)
Replace std::unordered_map with Abseil FlatHashMap or Swiss Table implementation (priority 1, 4-6% gain, low risk)
Add operator fusion and loop unrolling to scan-filter-aggregate loop (priority 5, 1-2% gain, low risk)
Implement AVX2 vectorized filtering for l_shipdate predicate (priority 2, 5-10% gain, medium risk)
strategy_notes: Apply optimizations in sequence: (1) Array-based aggregation first—this is the highest-impact, lowest-risk change that eliminates hash table overhead entirely for the degenerate 6-group case. (2) Compact hash table as fallback if cardinality is higher than expected. (3) Loop fusion and unrolling to expose ILP and reduce loop overhead. (4) SIMD vectorization on l_shipdate filter to process 8 comparisons in parallel on 59.99M rows. These four optimizations target different bottlenecks (lock contention, hash table latency, loop overhead, CPU utilization) and should be independently composable. Combined expected speedup: 15-25% (12-20 ms reduction). Target final time: 60-65 ms.
notes: Lock contention on global mutex during final aggregation merge will be naturally eliminated once array-based aggregation is implemented, as contention only occurs when multiple hash table updates collide. With direct array indexing, updates become lock-free atomic operations or simple array writes. Recommend measuring cache behavior after array impl to validate the 6-10% estimate. If SIMD vectorization encounters compiler issues, fallback to explicit -O3 -march=native compilation hints.
